ccel.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

title: Of the case of Fronto, healed by faith and prayer.

source.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

verses.text: Wherefore a man, Fronto by name, who was an officer of the Court and had a terrible disease, for he used to bite his own tongue and was in danger of injury to his eyes, having come to the mountain, asked Antony to pray for him.
But Antony said to him, ‘Depart and thou shalt be healed.’
But when he was violent and remained within some days, Antony waited and said, ‘If thou stayest here, thou canst not be healed.
Go, and having come into Egypt thou shalt see the sign wrought in thee.’
And as soon as he set eyes on Egypt his sufferings ceased, and the man became whole according to the word of Antony, which the Saviour had revealed to him in prayer.
